description: A retrospective public opinion analysis of the 1984-1985 UK miners' strike, which pitted the National Union of Mineworkers (NUM) led by Arthur Scargill against Margaret Thatcher's government over pit closures. The event resulted in a decisive defeat for the miners, fundamentally altering the UK industrial landscape and weakening trade union power for decades.

key_events.description: Arthur Scargill announces the national strike without a formal ballot of all miners.
The Battle of Orgreave occurs, leading to violent confrontations and significant negative media coverage for the miners.
The approach of winter increases public concern regarding potential power outages and coal shortages.
The NUM officially votes to return to work, marking the end of the year-long strike without concessions.

calibration_notes: This scenario tests the model's ability to handle high-stakes labor disputes where public sentiment shifts from initial sympathy to opposition due to economic inconvenience and aggressive union tactics.
